records.title: Our Balkan team and structure
records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000039, dated 1/14/1999, released in the Sep 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "Our Balkan team and structure". The released text begins: "Let's bring Jim Dobbins over from the NSC to replace Bob Gelbard SIRS and Special Representative of the President and Secretary of State as S/RS Stare for Balkan Peace issues (the exact title may still need some refinement). refinernent). Jim would be responsible for managing Dayton peace implementation, the Kosovo crisis, and all what I call war-and-peace issues in the former Yugoslavia."

records.title: December 16, 1996 letters From Strobe Talbott
records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000029, dated 12/16/1996, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 16, 1996 letters From Strobe Talbott". The released text begins: "Wheeler: It was a special pleasure to speak at the Dayton Town Meeting and reminisce about the many hours we worked last November to accomplish the signing of the Accords. The Department greatly appreciates the cooperation of the Air Force Association in arranging this conference."

records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000030, dated 12/4/1996, released in the Apr 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 4, 1996 Note FROM STROBE TALBOTT, IN OSLO TO THE SECRETARY OF STATE - ALL COPIES TO THE SECRETARY". The released text begins: "Except for Albania, this the only country in Europe I've never visited before. records.title: December 21, 1998 Letter From To Yuri
records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000026, dated 12/21/1998, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 21, 1998 Letter From To Yuri". The released text begins: "Igor Sergeyevich and I were seated next to each other at the Bosnia Peace Implementation Conference in Madrid, so we had a bit of a chance, amidst other business, to compare impressions. As you can imagine, these past few days since I returned from Moscow and Madrid have been pretty hectic, hence the delay in my getting back to you with some initial reactions to the ideas I heard in Moscow about CFE."

records.title: Handwritten Note w/Attachment(s)
records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000027, dated 12/3/1997, released in the Apr 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "Handwritten Note w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "The current debate over NATO's role in Bosnia, and its implications for the Alliance's future, however, leads me to hazard a few comments based upon my peacekeeping and NATO experience. The Alliance's essential function, it seems to me, is to link American power to European security, counterbalancing that of Russia, and thereby maintaining an equilibrium among Europe's major powers."

records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000044, dated 10/7/1998, released in the Apr 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "Weekly Report from the Department of State". The released text begins: "KEY DEPARTMENT NEWS • (U) Kosovo: UN Secretary General Kofi Annan issued a report October 5 on the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia's (FRY) compliance with UN Security Council Resolutions 1160 and 1199 related to Kosovo. Despite the Serb Prime Ministez's claim that peace prevails in Kosovo and that armed activities ceased on September 28, the report concludes that the violence there continued unabated in the week following adoption of UNSCR 1199 -- despite…"

records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000020, dated 4/28/1999, released in the June 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS ON RUSSIA/KOSOVO DIPLOMACY". The released text begins: "ALBRIGHT AND APNSA BERGER AND MP OVP FlIBRTH FUERTH ON BERLIN) FROM STROBE TALBOTT (IN SUBJECT: RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS ON RUSSIA/KOSOVO DIPLOMACY Moscow is the eye of a diplomatic storm this week, and the forecast calls for more of the sam… In some ways, better Moscow than Belgrade, since flying envoys tend to get in the way of other aircraft with work to do in the skies over Serbia."

records.title: International Peace Plan for Kosovo
records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000037, dated 5/15/1999, released in the June 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "International Peace Plan for Kosovo". The released text begins: "This would create conditions for international community for contacts with Yugoslav leadership to get their consent for the implementation of the international International Peace Plan. When the FRY leadership commits itself to the implementation of international peace plan described below and proceeds to implement its obligations, NATO would cease combat military activities directed against the FRY."

records.title: Kosovo DC Discussion on Use of Force; September 4; 2:00 pm; the White House
records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000029, dated 9/2/1998, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "Kosovo DC Discussion on Use of Force; September 4; 2:00 pm; the White House". The released text begins: "In our judgement, without a credible threat of force in Kosovo, it is unlikely that the international community will be able to leverage the kind of concessions out of Milosevic that are needed to come to closure on a political settlement. The uncertainty that surrounds NATO's role in Kosovo is severely limiting the influence that NATO planning exerts on the political track and damaging the credibility of the Alliance."

records.title: Fax From Strobe Talbott To Ambassador Holbrooke w/Attachment(s)
records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000013, dated 2/9/1998, released in the Oct 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "Fax From Strobe Talbott To Ambassador Holbrooke w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "After some further reflection, I've zeroed in on a theme that will be -— at least when fleshed out with specifics -— rather controversial. I'd like to seize two bulls by the horns: Russia/NATO and Turkey/EU."

records.title: Under Secretary Pickering's Meeting with Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Georgiy Mamedov, April 9, 1999, Dresden, Germany w/Attachment(s)
records.description: State Department FOIA record C09000042, dated 4/13/1999, released in the June 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "Under Secretary Pickering's Meeting with Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Georgiy Mamedov, April 9, 1999, Dresden, Germany w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "The conversations with Mamedov discussion centered on efforts to craft an approach on Kosovo that could bring NATO and Russia together. Basic elements would be a G8 ministerial that could endorse introduction of a Chapter VII resolution in the UNSC."
